concerns.text: T012a (Mock DFTB+) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but is described as a fallback triggered ONLY if T011 returns MISSING. It is a conditional branch of the data acquisition flow, not an independent parallel task. Marking it [P] implies it can run concurrently with T010/T011, which violates the logical dependency where it must wait for the failure signal from T011.

T016 (Data Integrity) lists dependencies on T012a, T010, T012, and T011. However, T012a is only executed if T011 fails. If T011 succeeds (real data), T012a never runs, making T016's dependency on T012a a logical error that would block execution in the 'real data' path. The dependency should be conditional or T012a should be decoupled from the main integrity check flow.

T014 (Hold-Out Mode) is marked [S] (Sequential) and depends on T013. However, the task description states it is part of the generation process 'if T012 is triggered'. T012 is the fallback logic. This creates a circular or ambiguous dependency: T014 depends on T013, but T013 (Primary Mode) is described as running in parallel with T010/T011. If T013 runs in parallel, T014 cannot strictly wait for T013 to complete before T012 (fallback) logic is even evaluated. The flow for generating the hold-out set is ambiguous relative to the fallback trigger.

The 'Sequential Execution Note' at the end of Phase 3 contradicts the task markers. It states 'T013 and T014 are executed as part of the generation process if T012 is triggered', but T013 is marked [P] (parallel) and T010/T011 are also [P]/[S]. The note implies a strict linear sequence (T010 -> T011 -> T012 -> T013 -> T014) that conflicts with the parallel markers assigned to T010 and T013, creating confusion about the actual execution graph.
